folio.label: 34v
folio.l: As blak he was , as ony cole or crowe ,
So was the blode , I ronnen in his face
¢ A non he was born~ , ou©t of the place
With hert sore , to Theseus paleys
Tho was he coruen , ou©t of his harneys
And in a bed I brougút , ful fair and blyue
For he was ©et , in memorie and lyue
And alwey criying , after Emelye
Duke Theseus , with al his companye
Is comen hom , to Athenes his Citee
With aÂ blisse , and gret solempnyte
Al be it , that this auenture was falle
He nolde nat , discomforten hem alle
Men seide eke , Arcite shal nat deye
He shal ben heled , of his maladye
And of a noæä thyng , they were as fayn
That of hem alle , was non I slayn
Alle were they sore I hurt , and namely on
That w a spere was thirled , æorugú æe brest bon
To oæere woundes , and to broken armes
So~me hadden salue , & so~me hadden charmes
Fermacies of herbes , and eke saue
They dronken , for æei wold here lyues have
For which this noble Duke , as he wel can
Comfortetú , and honoåeth , euäy man
And mad reuel , al the longe nygút
Vn to the straunge lordes , as was rigút
¢ Ne there was holden , non discomfityng
But as a Iustes , or a turneyeng
For sothly there was , no disconfiture
For fallyng nys nat , but an auenture
Ne to ben had by force , vn to the stake
Vn©olden , and with , xx é , knygútes take
O ¹sone a lone , with outen mo
And haried fortú , by arm foot and too
And eke his steede , dreuen fortú w staues
With fotmen , bothe ©emen & eke knaues
It nas a retted hi~ , no velanye
There may no man , clepe it cowardie
¢ For whicú a non , Duke Theseus let crie
To stynten al rancour , and Envie
The gree as wele , of o side as of other
And either side elyke , as others brother
And ©af he~ ©iftes , after here degree
And fully held a fest , dayes thre
And conueyed the kyngis , worthily
